---
title: "How It Works"
toc: false
---

<section class="page-lead">
<div class="eyebrow">From baseball data to a readable decision</div>
<h1>One pipeline, five questions.</h1>
<p>The system is designed to make the reasoning inspectable. Each stage answers a different question before a row is allowed onto the board.</p>
</section>

<div class="method-step"><div class="method-number">1</div><div><h3>What happened?</h3><p>Completed games, box scores, first-inning outcomes, venues, and game-time weather come from MLB's public Stats API.</p></div></div>
<div class="method-step"><div class="method-number">2</div><div><h3>What was knowable beforehand?</h3><p>Rolling player, opponent, team, starter, and venue features use only games before the target game. Current-game outcomes never enter their own inputs.</p></div></div>
<div class="method-step"><div class="method-number">3</div><div><h3>Does context improve prediction?</h3><p>Baseline and context-enhanced models are compared on later dates. Extra factors are selected only when they improve probability performance.</p></div></div>
<div class="method-step"><div class="method-number">4</div><div><h3>What does the market imply?</h3><p>FanDuel prices are converted from American odds to probabilities, then normalized across both sides to estimate a no-vig market probability.</p></div></div>
<div class="method-step"><div class="method-number">5</div><div><h3>Is the disagreement useful?</h3><p>The board ranks model probability minus market probability. Rows below the research threshold stay off the main board.</p></div></div>

## A simple example

<div class="callout-panel">
<span class="card-kicker">Illustration only</span>
<h3>Model: 58% · Market: 51% · Edge: +7%</h3>
<p>The model thinks the selected side happens 58 times in 100 similar situations. The no-vig market estimate is 51 times in 100. That seven-point difference is a strong signal to investigate. It is not a claim that the bet will win.</p>
</div>

## Pick Tracking

<p>The My Picks tab stores user-selected singles and parlays locally in the browser. The site refresh also publishes compact final-game settlement rows so saved slips can be marked pending, won, or lost after public MLB results are available. This is research tracking only: no stake size, payout, account, or payment information is collected.</p>
